scene_summary: A minimal expressionist architectural paper plate: pale gridded drafting field, one large folded plan mass with a triangular interior void, a dark wood threshold at the lower right, a separate small folded preview slab on the right, and sparse pinholes/seams along the left margin.
usable_surfaces: upper tiny archive ticks and glints

detected_objects.label: central plan aperture
detected_objects.visual_evidence: oversized folded drafting-paper footprint in the center with visible plan lines, cut shadows, curled edges, and a large interrupted architectural surface

detected_objects.label: mist-chamber edge slab
detected_objects.visual_evidence: separate pale translucent house-like folded slab at the right edge with soft shadow and faint internal plan traces

detected_objects.label: posed triangular void one
detected_objects.visual_evidence: large triangular negative opening inside the central folded footprint with a lit pale floor and sharp diagonal edge

detected_objects.label: posed curled overlay
detected_objects.visual_evidence: curled translucent paper band rising through the central plan, with rounded top curl, diagonal sheet face, and lower curled lip

detected_objects.label: lower brace notch
detected_objects.visual_evidence: dark reddish wood threshold slab at the lower right of the central footprint, interrupted by a vertical end block/notch

detected_objects.label: posed curled overlay
detected_objects.visual_evidence: Planned source surface fallback used because plate inspection returned fewer mapped objects than source bindings.

complexity_assessment.rationale: The plate is sparse and controlled: one dominant folded central plan, one right preview slab, one left seam, and a few tiny pin/glint marks. Negative space remains broad and legible.

mapping_note: Package assembly filled unmapped source pockets from planned artifact surfaces because plate inspection returned too few detected objects.
